title: Census in West Bengal to start from August 1: CM Suvendu
content: The Chief Minister said that West Bengal needs to catch up with other states in conduct of the census; he expressed concern over demographic changes and said census data will help in policy decisions

title: Heat wave to ease from Sunday as rain likely in parts of Andhra Pradesh
content: Temperatures may continue to hover around 45°C to 46°C on Saturday in several districts, while the IMD forecasts heavy rain, thunderstorms and strong winds over South Coastal Andhra Pradesh and Rayalaseema during the weekend

title: No one will dare breach our borders once new security grid is in place: Amit Shah
content: Home Minister said the government is planning to assign additional areas of responsibility to the BSF; noted that the most significant gap in the security grid lay in the incomplete fencing along the Bengal boundary

title: Naidu orders Zero FIR mechanism to curb cyber crime
content: Chief Minister directs immediate registration of complaints through proposed cyber war room to enable swift bank intervention, while unveiling a multi-pronged strategy to tackle online fraud and digital arrest scams

title: Supreme Court gives in-service teachers time till August 2028 to get TET qualification
content: SC says there will be no further extension; rejects arguments that TET mandate is a retrospective implementation of RTE Act; says teachers’ service cannot come at the cost of children’s educational future

title: Operation to locate terrorists in forests of Rajouri enters seventh day
content: Soldiers are continuing their pursuit of a group of terrorists reported to be holed up in the forests of Rajouri, in the Pir Panjal Valley, for the seventh straight day on Friday

title: RSS-linked tribal body seeks changes in ST status of converted tribals
content: According to JSM representatives, of India’s tribal population of nearly 12 crore recorded in the 2011 Census, an estimated 1.5 crore to 2 crore had converted to Christianity

title: CM inaugurates annual convention of ‘Snehagamam’
content: The event was organised by the Ranni-Nilackal diocese of the Malankara Mar Thoma Syrian Chruch to commemorate the golden jubilee of priesthood of Joseph Mar Barnabas Suffragan Metropolitan and Isaac Mar Philoxenos Episcopa

title: Senior IPS officer Mahesh Kumar Aggarwal appointed Head of the Tamil Nadu Police
content: The 1994-Batch officer is serving as the DGP in the Border Security Force, Delhi. He replaces DGP/HoPF Sandeep Rai  Rathore. He was in the panel of three IPS officers recommended by the Union Public Service Commission as fit for appointment to the top post

title: India’s policy on Myanmar will be pragmatic, says official
content: Criticism aimed at Indian policymakers for engaging Myanmar’s President U Min Aung Hlaing will not deter Delhi from forging ties with the military-backed regime, he says

title: Liquor buyers allege overcharging despite Karnataka govt.’s price cut
content: The State government introduced a new excise duty regime on May 11, under which alcohol is taxed based on Alcohol in Beverage content rather than category

title: Delhi HC upholds TRAI regulations capping television ads to 12 minutes per hour
content: Court says broadcasters cannot claim an ‘unfettered right to exploit spectrum for commercial purposes’, adding that ‘excessive or uneven commercial intrusion is a direct impairment of the right of consumers to a fair and reasonable viewing experience’

title: Hectic parleys in Delhi over ministry formation; CLP to meet today
content: Governor Thaawarchand Gehlot on Friday accepted the resignation of Chief Minister Siddaramaiah and has asked him to continue as the caretaker chief minister till the next government is formed

title: BJP keen to counter an aggressive D.K. Shivakumar in his own style
content: The saffron party is also hoping that there will be issues of conflict between supporters of Siddaramaiah and DKS that they can leverage

title: 15 dead in Pune, Pimpri-Chinchwad after drinking spurious liquor; eight arrested
content: Maharashtra CM orders police commissioners to conduct a joint probe; raids conducted at multiple liquor shops overnight; Opposition leaders allege failure in law enforcement, accuse police of a cover-up

title: Veteran Congress leader Gade Venkat Reddy passes away at 89
content: Venkat Reddy will be remembered for his his pivotal role in the Delimitation of Parliament and Assembly constituencies and his contributions during the Telangana statehood deliberations

title: AAP wins most wards in Punjab local polls, Opposition alleges misuse of govt. machinery
content: Punjab Chief Minister Bhagwant Mann says AAP’s victory in the municipal elections is a decisive endorsement of his government’s development-driven governance model

title: OMCs asked to maintain LPG reserves to meet at least thirty days of demand: Official
content: Officials stated that India’s domestic production of LPG presently stands at about 72,000 metric tonnes per day.

title: IMD lowers monsoon forecast, raises spectre of drought
content: IMD has put the probability of a ‘deficient’ monsoon (rain under 90% of the LPA) at 60%; only the Northeast is forecast to get ‘normal’ monsoon

title: Upalokayukta directs officers to implement public schemes effectively
content: K.N. Phaneendra, who is on a tour of Yadgir, pays surprise visits to several places, particularly the bus stand and public offices

title: Manoj Bajpayee, Huma Qureshi, Kritika Kamra to headline Hindu Huddle sessions in Bengaluru
content: Actors will discuss their journeys, roles, evolving craft, and changing landscape of Indian entertainment

title: Supreme Court says NEET paper leak traumatised entire families; points to ‘ad-hocism’ in NTA
content: The NTA filed an affidavit in the apex court over the corrective steps taken following NEET-UG paper leak and said the decision to cancel the examination was taken in the interest of students and in recognition of the trust underpinning the national examination system

title: Ebola outbreak: India has sent first tranche of medical supplies to Africa CDC, says MEA
content: On May 17, the World Health Organization (WHO) declared the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda a Public Health Emergency of International Concern.

title: Vishnu Vardhan takes charge as Director of Medical Education
content: He promises to ensure quality medical education, adequate infrastructure and efficient administration in government medical colleges and promote medical research

title: FIFA World Cup 2026 | Why football’s most passionate fans may be in India
content: In Kerala, West Bengal and several other places across India, the FIFA World Cup is a homecoming — of fan clubs, midnight screenings, bike rallies and WhatsApp groups that spring back to life every four years

title: Namma Metro services to be suspended for two hours on Purple Line stretch on May 31
content: According to a press release, metro train services between Deepanjali Nagar and Krantiveera Sangolli Rayanna Metro stations will remain suspended from 7 a.m. to 9 a.m. on May 31

title: 114 institutions allotted land in Amaravati so far, says MA&UD Minister Narayana
content: A total of 1,269 acres has been allotted to 70 government and 44 private organisations, which include banks, sports bodies, hotels, and medical and educational institutions, he says

title: Karimnagar jewellery heist mastermind remanded in judicial custody
content: The Karimnagar Police took gangster Subodh Singh into custody on a Prisoner Transit (PT) warrant in Bihar on Thursday

title: Udhayanidhi Stalin asks why launch of Singappen Special Task Force was put off
content: Udhayanidhi Stalin said the murder of a 25-year-old woman in Cuddalore was shocking and the recurring incidents of crime had raised serious questions about the law-and-order situation in the State

title: Karnataka government formation highlights: Congress to convene crucial CLP meeting on May 30
content: “Nothing decided yet, we have called CLP meeting tomorrow”: D.K. Shivakumar on new leadership in Karnataka

title: Update on status of Naga hostages in four days: Manipur students’ body to govt.
content: The All Naga Students’ Association, Manipur also sets a similar timeline for the abrogation of ceasefire deals with Kuki extremist groups

title: Watch: Full song or not? ‘Vande Mataram’ row erupts in Kerala | Above the Fold | 29.05.2026
content: In today’s epsiode Kerala’s ‘Vande Mataram’ controversy to fresh NEET-UG reforms, Vinesh Phogat’s Asian Games trials relief, IMD’s lowest monsoon forecast, Shah’s Gujarat border visit, Israel’s move against the UN chief, and more.

title: NCP(SP) splits in Kerala, Saseendran and  Thomas to float new outfit
content: The decision to form a new outfit was due to NCP (SP) leadership’s growing ties with the BJP-led NDA, especially during the recent Baramati Assembly bypoll in Maharashtra, says  Thomas K. Thomas. The new party aims to remain aligned with the LDF and is set to hold its first general body meeting on June 20.

title: Nandyal district plans ₹180 crore solar push under PM Surya Ghar scheme: Collector
content: District aims to add 39 MW of solar capacity and expand clean energy access in rural areas; 12 villages in Allagadda identified for development as solar villages with priority for SC and ST communities

title: Supreme Court refuses to interfere with anticipatory bail to Swami Avimukteshwaranand in POCSO case
content: During the proceedings, Justice Sundresh raised questions regarding the delay in approaching the police, noting that the complainant had claimed to possess information about the alleged incidents earlier.

title: Engineering institutions must nurture innovators, nation builders with ethical values: Vice-President
content: C.P. Radhakrishnan was speaking after inaugurating the Platinum Jubilee celebrations of UBDT College of Engineering in Davangere

title: Over 90% of Indian babies born in hospitals, 87% of one-year-olds fully vaccinated: NFHS-6
content: Survey conducted in 2023-2024 found reduced stunting, severe wasting among young children, improvements in services and nutrition for pregnant women, progress towards universal vaccination

title: Sidda meets Cong high command in Delhi a day after resignation - what’s on his 'wishlist'
content: Outgoing Karnataka CM Siddaramaiah met with Congress leaders in Delhi, fulfilling his promise to resign and paving the way for a transition. He reportedly sought cabinet berths for his son and loyalists, while the party prepares for a state unit reorganization where he is expected to play a key role.

title: A Note of Discord: US divided on proposed Trump $250 bill
content: America is considering a $250 bill featuring Donald Trump's face for its 250th birthday, a move that bypasses a law against living presidents on currency. Designs are prepared, sparking debate and satire. This proposal, alongside a UFC event on Trump's birthday, highlights the nation's deep divisions as its semiquincentennial approaches.

title: Eliminating language, culture & faith: Xi's repression of Uyghurs enters new phase
content: China's Xinjiang crackdown shifts focus from camps to identity, language, and religion. Despite claims of re-education graduates, a vast network of prisons, surveillance, and boarding schools persists. Uyghur culture faces suppression through education and labor programs, sparking renewed international concern over alleged crimes against humanity.

title: Hundreds of Wikipedia editors are threatening to go on strike and the reason is this
content: Hundreds of Wikipedia editors are threatening a potential strike after the Wikimedia Foundation disbanded its Community Tech team. Volunteers fear this move will leave community-requested tools and technical support neglected, potentially impacting the platform's daily operations and content maintenance.

title: 16-year-old student found hanging in Lucknow hostel; suicide note found
content: A 16-year-old student was found dead in a Lucknow hostel, with police investigating after her family allegedly tried to move the body for cremation without informing authorities. A note reading “I quit” was found in her room, considered a potential suicide note. The post-mortem and forensic reports are awaited.

title: Suspense over Vinesh Phogat’s weight category ahead of Asian Games trials
content: Wrestler Vinesh Phogat's participation in Asian Games trials is confirmed by courts, but her weight category remains a dispute. While Phogat desires to compete in 53kg, the WFI insists on 50kg, citing her "Iconic Athlete" status linked to her previous 50kg Olympic campaign. The final decision on her category is pending.

title: Four lines that 'layoff email' from Meta Leadership has for those on H-1B visa
content: Meta has initiated another round of significant layoffs, impacting nearly 8,000 employees globally, with notifications starting in Singapore. While many are let go, over 7,000 will transition to AI initiatives. The company is providing immigration guidance and severance packages, including 16 weeks' pay plus two weeks per service year, and 18 months of COBRA coverage for affected staff.

title: How government aims to save billions in forex with ethanol blended petrol
content: Ethanol-blended petrol is made by mixing ethanol with petrol. Ethanol is a biofuel that is derived from a wide variety of food crops and feed stocks like sugarcane and petrol. Under the Ethanol Blended Petrol Programme, the original target for achieving E20 petrol sales.

title: SP woman leader dragged by hair, 'beaten' inside house in UP; CCTV captures chilling attack
content: A Samajwadi Party leader in Chandauli has been brutally attacked inside her home. Gargi Singh Patel alleges her business associate's family assaulted her with iron rods. She claims the attack was part of a political conspiracy to harm her career. CCTV footage reportedly captured the incident. Police are investigating the assault and the alleged political links.

title: Amazon shuts down 'employee AI leadership board' & the reason is rising costs
content: Amazon has reportedly deactivated an internal AI usage leaderboard after employees "tokenmaxxing" to inflate their scores, leading to increased computing costs. The move highlights growing concerns for tech companies about rising AI expenses, as Amazon shifts focus to "normalised deployments" measuring useful code creation over raw token consumption.

title: Sooryavanshi equals Maxwell’s 12-year-old IPL record with stunning 96-run blitz
content: Teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi smashed 96 off 47 balls in IPL 2026 Qualifier 2 against Gujarat Titans, narrowly missing a century for the second consecutive knockout game. The 15-year-old equalled Glenn Maxwell's record for most dismissals in the 90s in a season and became the fastest to 1000 runs by balls faced.

title: CBSE OSM row: 20 answer-sheet mix-ups detected; over 13K copies evaluated manually - report
content: Central Board of Secondary Education's new digital evaluation system encountered problems. Around 20 answer sheets were mixed up. Over 13,000 required manual checking due to poor scanning. This led to a delay in the post-result verification portal. The board is working to fix technical glitches and ensure a smooth process for students.

title: Ryan Clark slammed over Josh Jacobs silence amid Jaxson Dart comments
content: The Pivot podcast host Ryan Clark recently commented on Jaxson Dart introducing Donald Trump. He emphasized the impact on locker room dynamics and supported Abdul Carter. However, Clark remained silent on Josh Jacobs’ recent arrest for alleged domestic violence. Though they could be bound by strict policies to not comment on it. Robert Griffin III did. Clark’s silence drew criticism. Especially when a year-old tweet about Jacobs and Henry Ruggs III resurfaced online the same time Jacobs’ case was running wild on cyberspace. The post highlighted Clark’s past engagement with Jacobs.

title: 'Entitled brat': Hasan Piker faces backlash as Ana Kasparian defends him
content: Ana Kasparian publicly denied Dave Rubin’s claims that Hasan Piker harassed her while they were connected to The Young Turks. Kasparian called the allegations false and said Piker should sue Rubin for defamation. Rubin also criticized Piker’s career background and political views, but Kasparian’s statement became the main focus of the controversy and online discussion.

title: Bruce Cassidy update sparks Oilers-Golden Knights coaching buzz
content: Bruce Cassidy’s NHL future remains a hot topic following some new comments from Vegas Golden Knights general manager Kelly McCrimmon. The Edmonton Oilers are still very much in the running to hire the veteran coach, while Vegas continues to deny interview requests. With more than one team watching this unfold and league-wide reactions building, the coaching saga is rapidly becoming among the biggest storylines of the NHL’s offseason.

title: Why the Strait of Hormuz worries China more than Malacca
content: A new report highlights China's key maritime vulnerability lies at the Strait of Hormuz, not the Malacca Strait. This shifts strategic competition in the Indian Ocean to a new arena involving India, France, and the US. China's energy imports through this region expose it to potential exploitation by rival powers.

title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi creates IPL history, becomes fastest ever to...
content: Rajasthan Royals' Vaibhav Sooryavanshi shattered records in Qualifier 2, becoming the fastest to 1000 IPL runs, surpassing Andre Russell.  Despite early wickets and a teammate's injury, the teenager's explosive 96 off 47 balls, featuring eight fours and seven sixes, propelled RR's innings and added to his historic IPL 2026 campaign.

title: Kaal Bhairava goes to Europe: Why Portugal deal is gamechanger for India’s defence sector
content: An Indian-designed autonomous combat aircraft entering the European manufacturing ecosystem is an endeavour Indian defence planners seemingly wanted for years. But beyond the symbolism lies a far bigger story involving military technology, Nato interoperability, artificial intelligence, export controls, defence geopolitics and the rapidly changing nature of modern warfare. The agreement also highlights something increasingly visible in global defence manufacturing as the military power is no longer built entirely inside geographical limits.

title: ‘Everyone is equal before God’: VIP darshan in temples wrong & discriminatory, says Madras HC
content: The bench rejected a submission made by the state’s additional advocate-general, P V Balasubramaniam, that withdrawing paid VIP darshan would cause revenue loss to the temples.

title: 'Referring wides in cricket needs to stop': Kevin Pietersen sparks fresh IPL debate
content: Former England captain Kevin Pietersen sparked debate during IPL 2026 Qualifier 2 after criticising wide-ball referrals in cricket. “Referring wides in cricket needs to stop,” Pietersen posted on X while watching Gujarat Titans vs Rajasthan Royals. His remark divided fans, with some saying reviews slow down T20 games, while others defended technology for ensuring accurate decisions in high-pressure matches.

title: Ex-Trump attorney general grilled over Epstein files amid cancer diagnosis
content: Former US attorney general Pam Bondi appeared before the House Oversight Committee. She admitted to redaction errors in the Jeffrey Epstein files. Bondi defended the Justice Department's actions under her leadership. Democrats and survivors criticized the closed-door interview format. They demanded greater transparency and accountability in the handling of these sensitive documents.

title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi achieves never-seen-before IPL feat in Qualifier 2
content: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi has achieved a historic IPL milestone. The Rajasthan Royals teenager is the first uncapped player to score 700 runs in a single season. He also set a T20 record for most powerplay runs in a tournament. Sooryavanshi's incredible form continues to break records. His season now stands as the highest-scoring IPL campaign by an uncapped player.

title: Mini-pigs, lab-grown organs, frozen chambers: How Putin wants to live forever
content: Russia is investing billions in a state-backed initiative focused on longevity, exploring gene therapy, organ printing, and cryotherapy. This ambition, fueled by President Putin's personal fascination with extending life and power, aims to overcome aging. Despite bold claims, critics question the scientific validity, while Russia grapples with low life expectancy.

title: 'ED party has been wiped out': Kejriwal mocks BJP after AAP sweeps local body poll in Punjab
content: Aam Aadmi Party secured a significant victory in Punjab's urban local body elections, prompting AAP chief Arvind Kejriwal to declare the "ED party" (BJP) wiped out. Kejriwal accused the BJP of harassing traders, stating people had taken revenge.

title: Bloodbath on D-Street: Investors lose Rs 5.77 lakh cr in final hours of trade today
content: Indian stock markets experienced a significant downturn on Friday. Investor wealth saw a substantial decline of Rs 5.77 lakh crore. Benchmark indices, Sensex and Nifty50, plunged sharply in the final trading hour. Concerns over a weaker monsoon outlook and geopolitical uncertainty fueled the sell-off. Broad-based selling pressure was observed across various sectors.

title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi scripts history, becomes first player ever to...
content: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi has made IPL history. He is the first player in T20 cricket to score 500 runs in the powerplay during a single tournament. This happened in IPL 2026. He achieved this milestone in a crucial qualifier match. Rajasthan Royals relied on him after early wickets. Sooryavanshi has been a standout performer all season.

title: How to destroy drones electronically, without firing bullets
content: Drones are revolutionizing warfare, with their cost-effectiveness making them ubiquitous. Counter-drone systems are evolving rapidly, increasingly relying on non-kinetic methods like lasers and EMPs to neutralize threats. Advanced detection and identification technologies, including AI and sophisticated sensors, are crucial in this escalating technological arms race.

title: How Pant’s Rs 27 crore LSG move turned into an IPL disaster in just two seasons
content: Rishabh Pant has stepped down as Lucknow Super Giants captain after two disappointing IPL seasons. Bought for a record Rs 27 crore, Pant's tenure saw LSG finish bottom in IPL 2026, extending their playoff drought. His individual form also struggled to justify the hefty price tag, impacting his national team prospects.

title: How DK Shivakumar’s school-day battles shaped him to be Karnataka’s next CM
content: From childhood declarations of destiny to early electoral setbacks, D K Shivakumar's political journey is marked by a relentless drive to lead. Repeated denials in school only fueled his determination, teaching him to strategize and ensure victory, even from behind the scenes. This resilience and ability to absorb opponents became foundational to his rise.

title: Sonakshi Sinha rents out Bandra luxury home to Kuwait Consulate: Report
content: Sonakshi Sinha has leased out her upscale apartment in Mumbai’s Bandra West to the Consulate General of the State of Kuwait for a yearly rent of Rs 1.92 crore, property registration documents accessed by CRE Matrix revealed. The apartment has reportedly been rented for the residential use of Kuwait’s Consul General Emad Abdul Aziz Al-Kharaz along with his family members and staff. The agreement states that the monthly rent for the property is Rs 16 lakh. The lease period begins on June 7, 2026, and will continue till June 6, 2027.

title: Watch: Gukesh in splits after Javokhir Sindarov asked to sing on his B-day
content: D Gukesh enjoyed a funny birthday moment during Norway Chess 2026 celebrations as players sang for his 20th birthday before a sailing event in Oslo. Laughter erupted after Wesley So jokingly asked Javokhir Sindarov, Gukesh’s future World Championship challenger, to sing a song. Earlier, Magnus Carlsen defeated Gukesh in Round 4, while Bibisara Assaubayeva continued leading the women’s standings.

title: Flight preponed without notice, court orders Rs 50,000 compensation with ticket refund
content: Two friends were left stranded in Bali after Vistara preponed their return flight without notice. Forced to buy new tickets, they missed connecting flights, incurring further costs. A consumer commission has now ordered the airline to pay Rs 50,000 compensation, a full ticket refund with interest, and Rs 15,000 in litigation costs, deeming the initial award inadequate for their ordeal.

title: 'Making a fuss': Kremlin dismisses Nato fury over Russian drone crash in Romania
content: A Russian drone crashed into an apartment building in eastern Romania, injuring two people and sparking outrage. President Nicusor Dan called it the "worst incident to hit the national territory" since the war began. NATO and EU leaders condemned the "serious and irresponsible escalation," with Romania requesting faster anti-drone aid. This occurred amidst renewed Russian attacks on Ukraine.

title: Drama before first ball! RR win controversial re-toss against GT in Qualifier 2
content: Confusion reigned at the toss for the Rajasthan Royals vs. Gujarat Titans match, necessitating a re-flip. Rajasthan captain Riyan Parag's initial call was deemed unclear, leading to a second toss which he again won, opting to bat. Gujarat's Shubman Gill also admitted a preference for batting first on the used pitch, anticipating it would slow down.

title: IPL 2026: Rishabh Pant steps down as Lucknow Super Giants captain
content: Rishabh Pant has stepped down as captain of the Lucknow Super Giants, a decision accepted immediately by the franchise. Pant himself initiated the request to be relieved of his leadership duties. The franchise expressed gratitude for his contributions and is now focused on rebuilding. This comes after Pant's record-breaking Rs 27 crore move to LSG.

title: TOI Sports Poll: Bumrah emerges top choice to lead MI; SKY least preferred
content: Following another disappointing IPL season, Mumbai Indians fans overwhelmingly favour pace spearhead Jasprit Bumrah to take over captaincy for IPL 2027. A poll revealed over 44% support for Bumrah, who has been a key player for the franchise. Rohit Sharma and Tilak Varma also received significant votes, while Suryakumar Yadav was least preferred.

title: Watch: Rahul Gandhi dons auto driver's uniform, shares meal and interacts with them in Delhi
content: Rahul Gandhi interacted with autorickshaw drivers in Delhi, listening to their concerns about rising expenses and livelihoods. He wore a driver's uniform and shared a simple meal with them, promising to raise their issues in Parliament. The outreach occurred as the Congress party prepares for discussions on Karnataka cabinet representation.

title: Pooja Singh breaks 14-yr-old NR, wins historic Asian U20 high jump gold — Watch
content: Teenage high jumper Pooja Singh made history at the Asian U20 Athletics Championships, clinching gold with a 1.93m leap. This stunning performance not only made her the first Indian woman to win this title but also shattered the 14-year-old senior National Record. Her achievement also meets the qualification standard for the Commonwealth Games 2026.

title: AI Cameras, QR Codes: Delhi's New Tech Push To Battle Dust Pollution
content: The Delhi Pollution Control Committee will launch "DUST Portal 2.0" next week, allowing authorities to monitor construction activity across the city round the clock.

title: Hormuz Is Shut, Indian Ships Still Passing. Centre Shares 'Secret' Strategy
content: The movement is significant because Iran holds a major geographical advantage over the Strait and has repeatedly targeted vessels in retaliation for the US-Israeli strikes on February 28 that triggered the war.

title: Infosys CEO's Salary Saw 2.5% Jump In 2026. This Is How Much He Earned
content: Salil Parekh took home a fixed salary of Rs 7.97 crore and bonuses worth Rs 2335 crore. The Infosys CEO exercising his stock options accounted for the lion's share of his compensation for the year.

title: Iran War LIVE Updates: Ships In Hormuz May Start Process Of "Heading Home": Trump
content: US Iran War LIVE Updates: US President Donald Trump had on Wednesday dismissed an Iranian state media report that Iran and Oman would jointly manage shipping through the Strait of Hormuz

title: 5-Month Planning, Stabbing, Burnt Car: How A Minor Killed 4 Family Members
content: What initially appeared to be an accident involving a burnt car has now turned into one of Rajasthan's most chilling murder investigations.

title: India's Forex Reserves Drop By $7.5 Billion To 681.38 Billion
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has also made multiple public appeals to countrymen to conserve forex by cutting down on foreign travel, limiting fuel use and refraining from gold buys for a year.

title: Weather Today LIVE: Rain, Cloud Cover Bring Welcome Relief In Delhi
content: Weather Updates Today, Heatwave, IMD Alert LIVE Updates: Strong winds and light rain bring relief from heat in Delhi with thunderstorms overnight.

title: 1,600 Strikes, 500 Drone Attacks: The 'Illusion' Of Israel-Lebanon Ceasefire
content: Since its announcement by the US, Israel and Hezbollah have traded heavy fire, and Israeli forces have continued to demolish villages in southern Lebanon.

title: "Get Ready," Russia Tells Romania To Brace For More Drone Incidents
content: Russian President Vladimir Putin had been informed about the drone incident in Romania, the TASS state news agency reported earlier on Friday.

title: Why Stock Market Crashed At 3 PM Today? Sudden Sensex Fall Explained
content: The India Meteorological Department's forecast of below-normal rainfall during the June-September monsoon season was one of the key triggers for Friday's selloff.

title: AAP Sweeps Punjab Civic Polls, Wins 862 Wards, Congress Distant Second
content: Chandigarh, May 29 (IANS) The ruling Aam Aadmi Party (AAP) in Punjab on Friday achieved an impressive victory in 102 civic bodies by winning 862 wards of the 1,765 that have been counted till now. A total of 1,977 wards went to the polls on May 26.

title: Kerala Governor Upset Over 'Incomplete' Vande Mataram, Chief Minister Replies
content: Before and after the Governor read out the UDF government's policy address in the Kerala Assembly, a band team performed the opening stanzas of Vande Mataram.

title: Liquor Hidden In Oxygen Tanker, Gujarat Cops Seize Alcohol Worth Rs 64 Lakh
content: Police have arrested the driver of the commercial tanker, identified as Rekharam Motaram Jat from Jalore, Rajasthan.

title: "They Walk Among Us": White House Launches "Aliens" Website. What It's About
content: The website is called Aliens.gov and is a space-themed immigration enforcement website that features a US map tracking "alien arrests". The data on the site will be updated and will also be included in the White House app.

title: 5 Delhi Water Sports Clubs Face Eviction In Action After Gymkhana Debate
content: According to the notice, the land near the inlet of the Sonia Vihar Water Treatment Plant is government-owned land that has been occupied through "temporary structures".

title: "Why Marry And Insult Bride, Her Family?" Top Court In Dowry Death Case
content: The case dates back to 2010, when a woman, within three years of her marriage, was found hanging at her marital home.

title: Driver Killed In Ambush On Manipur Highway, Cop And 5 Protesters Injured
content: The security forces had been clearing blockades on the highway since this morning amid protests by villagers

title: In Maharashtra MLC Poll Deal, Congress, Uddhav Sena Tussle Over 2 Seats
content: According to sources, the seat-sharing arrangement was carefully worked out to avoid friction among allies while balancing each party's regional strength and political influence.

content: satirical online outfit - the Cockroach Janata Party (CJP) . it was a 'misinterpreted' comparison of cockroaches to humans . the party's manifesto had demands for a ban on post-retirement seats .
